schema.appDescription: Calculate overtime pay, hourly rate, and total monthly salary including overtime. Based on Factories Act 1948 — OT must be paid at double the ordinary rate.

howToSteps.name: Enter your details
howToSteps.text: Fill in the input fields for the Overtime Calculator. Smart defaults are pre-filled.

howToSteps.name: View instant results
howToSteps.text: Results update in real time — no submit button needed.

howToSteps.name: Explore breakdown
howToSteps.text: Open the year-by-year breakdown to see numbers over time.

howToSteps.name: Compare scenarios
howToSteps.text: Adjust inputs to compare scenarios and find the best option.

faq.q: What is the overtime rate in India under labour law?
faq.a: The Factories Act 1948 mandates overtime pay at twice the ordinary rate for factory workers. Normal working hours: 48 hours/week (9 hours/day). Any work beyond this is overtime. The new Labour Codes retain this double-rate provision. IT/services employees typically follow company policy.

Yes. Overtime pay is fully taxable as part of salary income. There is no exemption for overtime earnings. TDS on overtime is calculated by your employer based on projected annual income. The tax is straightforward — overtime is just additional salary.

Most IT professionals are categorised as 'management staff' or 'skilled workers' exempt from overtime provisions. However, some IT companies pay OT voluntarily or offer comp-off (compensatory off) instead of payment. Start-ups often have no formal OT policy.
Yes, completely free on CalcPhi with no registration.
No. All calculations run in your browser. Your data is never stored.
Yes. Works on all modern smartphones and tablets.

examples.heading: ₹30,000 basic — 20 OT hours/month
examples.text: Hourly rate = 30,000 ÷ (26 × 8) = ₹144/hour. OT rate = ₹288/hour. 20 OT hours = ₹5,769. Total monthly = ₹35,769. Annual OT income = ₹69,228 (if consistent) — adds meaningful income for factory workers.

examples.heading: Shift worker calculation
examples.text: A factory worker earning ₹20,000 basic working 30 OT hours/month at ₹192/hour OT rate earns ₹5,769 OT pay. Total ₹25,769/month. At 12 months = ₹3,09,231 annual income — below taxable limit, so no TDS.
